summary.intent: Run a CEO-level review of CHA-19 (Web UI CRM Tab) to validate scope, architecture decisions, and implementation plan before moving to implementation.
summary.outcome: CEO review v2 was written, saved to plans/CHA-19-ceo-review-v2.md, posted as a Linear comment on CHA-19, and committed to the workspace branch.

repo: All page data access uses WebSocket RPC via sendRpc() — there are NO REST endpoints for page data in this codebase. The CEO review's REST recommendation was wrong.
RPC methods for CRM already exist at gateway/src/methods/services.rs:4516 — 14 methods covering full CRUD for contacts, matters, interactions, and channels.
The channels page (page-channels.js) is the canonical pattern for new page modules: signal state, sendRpc(), registerPrefix(), single large file with internal Preact components.
Feature gating uses #[cfg(feature = "crm")] in Rust and gon-injected booleans (e.g. graphql_enabled, voice_enabled) in JS to conditionally register routes.
NavCounts uses tokio::join! for concurrent queries; adding a CRM count adds ~1ms latency.
The web crate accesses CRM through gateway services (GatewayState.services.crm), not by depending on the crm crate directly.
i18n uses per-namespace locale files in locales/en|fr|zh/*.js registered via i18n.js.
SqliteCrmStore.list_filtered does NOT escape % and _ wildcards in LIKE search — cosmetic issue, low severity in single-tenant context.

code.finding: All 14 CRM RPC methods registered here: crm.contacts.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.channels.list/upsert/delete, crm.matters.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.interactions.list/record/delete
list_filtered() passes user search input directly into LIKE '%term%' without escaping % or _ wildcards
LiveCrmService.contact_to_json() deliberately calls expose_secret() on email/phone for JSON serialization — correct for authenticated single-tenant access

workflow: LINEAR_API_KEY is not available as an env var inside the agent sandbox — had to use Python with requests library to call the Linear API directly.

The plan-ceo-review skill produced two conflicting reviews (CEO recommended REST, Eng corrected to RPC). The second pass correctly used codebase exploration to override the first pass's wrong assumption.
Stokowski lifecycle context is injected via AUTO-GENERATED comment blocks in the prompt — these carry state (run number, prior workpad content, issue URL) across agent sessions.
Plans are persisted to stokowski-workspaces/<issue-id>/plans/ and committed to git, serving as durable cross-session artifacts.

friction: LINEAR_API_KEY not available in the sandbox environment — required fallback to Python requests instead of a CLI tool.

The initial CEO review (v1) recommended ~15 new REST handlers — a significant scope error that required a full eng review to correct. The CEO review skill did not explore the codebase before making architecture recommendations.
Two separate review files were produced (CHA-19-ceo-review.md and CHA-19-ceo-review-v2.md) — the v1 file is now stale/misleading but was not deleted.
open_items: E2E test implementation for CRM tab — deferred to separate ticket, requires gateway running with CRM feature enabled
Optimistic locking for concurrent CRM updates — last-write-wins is a known gap, deferred as acceptable for v1 single-user
LIKE wildcard escaping in SqliteCrmStore.list_filtered — % and _ pass through unescaped, P3 cosmetic issue deferred
Contact count optimization — NavCounts loads all contacts to count them; should use SELECT COUNT(*) at 1000+ contacts, deferred
Matter kanban drag-and-drop view — Phase 2 feature
PII reveal-on-click masking — polish item, deferred from v1
French and Chinese translations — locale files will be created with English fallback strings; proper translations are a separate i18n pass
Stale CHA-19-ceo-review.md (v1) in plans/ — contains wrong REST architecture recommendation, should be deleted or clearly marked superseded
